title: fomc / federal reserve / rate decision | Fomc Statement | Federal Reserve issues FOMC statement
summary: The Federal Open Market Committee voted 11-1 to maintain the federal funds rate target range at 3-1/2 to 3-3/4 percent at its March 18, 2026 meeting. The Committee noted solid economic growth, low job gains, and somewhat elevated inflation while emphasizing elevated uncertainty about the economic outlook, including uncertainty around Middle East developments. Stephen I. Miran was the sole dissenter, preferring a 25 basis point cut.
